tasks.title: Backend: extend renameSpace() to persist agentNicknames
tasks.description: Add agentNicknames parameter to spaceManager.renameSpace(). Implement normaliseNicknames() helper that drops empty-string entries and trims values. Validate each value is a string with trimmed length ≤ 50 chars (return VALIDATION_ERROR otherwise). Write the normalised map into the space object in spaces.json using the existing atomic write pattern.
acceptance_criteria: PUT /api/v1/spaces/:id with { agentNicknames: { 'senior-architect': 'El Jefe' } } returns 200 with the nickname included in the response body
Empty-string nickname values (e.g. { 'developer-agent': '' }) are stripped before write and absent from the response
Nickname value exceeding 50 chars returns 400 with code VALIDATION_ERROR
Existing spaces without agentNicknames continue to behave identically — GET returns space without the field
PUT with agentNicknames: undefined leaves the existing agentNicknames value unchanged (no regression)
Atomic write: no partial write state possible (tmp file rename pattern used)
Unit test: normaliseNicknames() drops empty strings, trims values, handles undefined input

tasks.title: Backend: pass agentNicknames through PUT route handler
tasks.description: In src/routes/index.js, extract agentNicknames from the parsed request body and pass it as the sixth argument to spaceManager.renameSpace(). Also update the POST /api/v1/spaces handler to NOT accept agentNicknames on create (it must only be settable via PUT on an existing space).
acceptance_criteria: PUT /api/v1/spaces/:id body with agentNicknames is correctly forwarded to renameSpace()
POST /api/v1/spaces ignores agentNicknames in the body — the created space never has agentNicknames set
Integration test: PUT a space with nicknames, GET the space, verify nicknames are present in the response

tasks.title: Frontend: add agentNicknames to Space type and api client
tasks.description: In frontend/src/types/index.ts, add agentNicknames?: Record<string, string> to the Space interface. In frontend/src/api/client.ts, extend the updateSpace() payload type to include agentNicknames?: Record<string, string> and pass it through to the PUT request body.
acceptance_criteria: Space interface has agentNicknames?: Record<string, string>
updateSpace() in api/client.ts accepts and forwards agentNicknames in the request body
TypeScript compiler reports zero new errors after these changes (npm run build in frontend/)
No other types or interfaces regress

tasks.title: Frontend: create agentName.ts utility with resolveAgentName and resolveAgentShortLabel
tasks.description: Create frontend/src/utils/agentName.ts. Implement resolveAgentName(agentId, space, agents?) using the four-step fallback chain: space.agentNicknames[agentId] → STAGE_DISPLAY[agentId] → agents[].displayName → agentId. Implement resolveAgentShortLabel(agentId, space) using: nickname truncated to 6 chars with ellipsis → STAGE_LABELS[agentId] → agentId.split('-')[0]. STAGE_DISPLAY and STAGE_LABELS constants are moved here from RunIndicator.tsx and StageTabBar.tsx (or imported from here by those files to avoid duplication).
acceptance_criteria: resolveAgentName('senior-architect', { agentNicknames: { 'senior-architect': 'El Jefe' } }) returns 'El Jefe'
resolveAgentName('senior-architect', null) returns 'Senior Architect' (static fallback)
resolveAgentName('unknown-agent', null) returns 'unknown-agent' (raw ID fallback)
resolveAgentShortLabel('senior-architect', { agentNicknames: { 'senior-architect': 'El Jefe' } }) returns 'El Jef…'
resolveAgentShortLabel('senior-architect', null) returns 'Arch' (static short label)
Unit tests cover all fallback levels for both functions
TypeScript types are correctly annotated — no any casts

tasks.title: Frontend: update useAppStore renameSpace action to pass agentNicknames
tasks.description: In frontend/src/stores/useAppStore.ts, extend the renameSpace action signature to accept agentNicknames?: Record<string, string> as a fifth optional parameter. Pass it through to api.updateSpace(). No new store state slice is needed — the nicknames are read from the spaces[] array which is already kept in sync after every updateSpace() call.
acceptance_criteria: renameSpace action signature accepts agentNicknames as fifth optional argument
Calling renameSpace without agentNicknames does not break existing behaviour
After a successful renameSpace call with agentNicknames, the spaces[] array in the store reflects the updated nicknames
TypeScript compilation clean

tasks.title: Frontend: add Agent Nicknames section to SpaceModal (rename mode only)
tasks.description: In frontend/src/components/modals/SpaceModal.tsx, add an 'Agent Nicknames' collapsible section inside ModalBody, rendered only when mode === 'rename'. The section shows one input row per agent in the resolved pipeline (space.pipeline ?? DEFAULT_STAGES). Each row has the agent ID as a label and a text input for the nickname (max 50 chars, placeholder 'e.g. El Jefe'). A 'Clear all' link resets all inputs to empty. Local state: nicknames (Record<string, string>), nicknamesOpen (boolean, default false). On open in rename mode, initialise from space.agentNicknames ?? {}. Pass nicknames to renameSpace() on submit.
acceptance_criteria: Nicknames section is NOT rendered when mode === 'create'
Nicknames section is collapsed by default when mode === 'rename'
Clicking the section header toggles it open/closed
Inputs are pre-filled from space.agentNicknames on modal open
Typing a nickname and clicking Save sends the updated nickname to the API
Clearing a nickname input (empty string) results in that entry being removed from space.agentNicknames
Nickname input longer than 50 chars shows an inline validation error and blocks submit
'Clear all' resets all nickname inputs to empty without closing the modal
Design system compliant: Tailwind tokens only, no inline styles, uses existing inputClass pattern
Accessible: each input has an associated label with htmlFor

tasks.title: Frontend: integrate resolveAgentName into RunIndicator
tasks.description: In frontend/src/components/agent-launcher/RunIndicator.tsx, replace the inline STAGE_DISPLAY lookup (lines ~532-534) and the PausedBanner stageName computation (line ~516) with calls to resolveAgentName(agentId, activeSpace, agents). The activeSpace is derived from useAppStore: spaces.find(s => s.id === activeSpaceId). Import resolveAgentName and resolveAgentShortLabel from @/utils/agentName. Remove the local STAGE_DISPLAY and STAGE_LABELS constants (or keep as fallback if they are used elsewhere in the file — check usages).
acceptance_criteria: When a space has agentNicknames: { 'senior-architect': 'El Jefe' }, the RunIndicator shows 'El Jefe' as the display name for that agent
When no nickname is set, existing static labels are used (no visual regression)
PausedBanner stageName correctly shows the nickname when set
No TypeScript errors introduced

tasks.title: Frontend: integrate resolveAgentShortLabel into StageTabBar
tasks.description: In frontend/src/components/pipeline-log/StageTabBar.tsx, replace the internal getShortLabel(agentId) call with resolveAgentShortLabel(agentId, activeSpace). The activeSpace must be passed as a prop from PipelineLogPanel (which already has access to the store). Update StageTabBar props interface accordingly.
acceptance_criteria: StageTabBar accepts an optional activeSpace prop of type Space | null
When activeSpace.agentNicknames has an entry for the agentId, the short label shows the truncated nickname (≤ 6 chars + ellipsis if needed)
When no nickname is set, existing short labels render identically to before
PipelineLogPanel passes activeSpace to StageTabBar

tasks.title: Frontend: integrate resolveAgentName into PipelineConfirmModal and TaskDetailPanel
tasks.description: In PipelineConfirmModal.tsx, replace raw agentId display in the stage list with resolveAgentName(agentId, activeSpace, agents). In TaskDetailPanel.tsx (line ~298), replace {agentId} text with resolveAgentName(agentId, activeSpace, agents). Both components already have access to activeSpace via the store. Also update SpaceModal.tsx stage selector options to use resolveAgentName for the displayed option text (create mode can use static labels since no space exists yet).
acceptance_criteria: PipelineConfirmModal stage list shows nicknames when set for the active space
TaskDetailPanel stage list shows nicknames when set for the active space
SpaceModal pipeline selector shows nicknames in rename mode
Falling back to static labels when no nickname is set — no visual regression

tasks.title: Tests: backend unit tests for agentNicknames in spaceManager
tasks.description: Add unit tests in the backend test suite (Node.js node:test runner) for the agentNicknames feature: normaliseNicknames helper, renameSpace with agentNicknames param, validation edge cases (value too long, non-string keys). Use temp directories as in existing spaceManager tests.
acceptance_criteria: Test: renameSpace with valid agentNicknames persists correctly
Test: renameSpace with empty-string nickname value drops that entry
Test: renameSpace with agentNicknames value > 50 chars returns VALIDATION_ERROR
Test: renameSpace without agentNicknames parameter leaves existing nicknames unchanged
Test: PUT route handler passes agentNicknames to renameSpace
npm test passes with zero failures

tasks.title: Tests: frontend unit tests for agentName.ts utility
tasks.description: Write Vitest unit tests for resolveAgentName and resolveAgentShortLabel covering all fallback levels and edge cases (null space, empty nickname map, nickname empty string, known and unknown agentIds).
acceptance_criteria: resolveAgentName: all four fallback levels tested
resolveAgentShortLabel: nickname truncation tested (nickname = 'El Jefe' → 'El Jef…'), static map fallback tested
Edge case: agentNicknames value = '' treated as absent (falls through to next level)
cd frontend && npm test passes with zero failures

tasks.title: Tests: component tests for SpaceModal nicknames section
tasks.description: Write React Testing Library tests for the new nicknames section in SpaceModal: section hidden in create mode, visible (collapsed) in rename mode, toggle open/close, pre-fill from space data, submit sends nicknames to renameSpace, clear all resets inputs, validation error for value > 50 chars.
acceptance_criteria: Nicknames section not present when mode=create
Nicknames section present but collapsed when mode=rename
Toggle chevron opens/closes the section
Inputs pre-filled from space.agentNicknames
Submitting with a nickname calls renameSpace with the correct agentNicknames map
Clear all empties all inputs
Input value > 50 chars blocks form submit and shows error
